title: The National Law Review
content: The National Law Review is a free-to-use, no-log-in database of legal and business articles.

title: The unfolding heat crisis in Thailand and Southeast Asia: Challenges and emerging solutions
content: This publication aims to raise awareness of the growing risks and impacts of heat stress and to provide practical guidance on regulatory, policy, and technical solutions that can help reduce its adverse effects.

title: Small but mighty — climate resilience in Europe’s small municipalities
content: This briefing showcases how Europe’s small municipalities are building climate resilience despite size-related challenges. It is based on a literature review, case studies and analysis of two EU-level databases.

title: Climate resilience in Europe, 2025 — progress and challenges
content: The objective of this report is to present an analysis of European progress towards climate resilience since 2021, with an emphasis on the state of play in 2025.
snippet: The objective of this report is to present an analysis of European progress towards climate resilience since 2021, with an emphasis on the state of play in 2025.

title: NASA lifelines community tool showcase
content: Join to explore how Earth observation data and geospatial tools can support disaster risk reduction and humanitarian decision-making across a range of use cases.
snippet: Join to explore how Earth observation data and geospatial tools can support disaster risk reduction and humanitarian decision-making across a range of use cases.

title: Quillette
content: Quillette is an Australian-based but internationally-focused online magazine that specialises in long-form analysis and cultural commentary.

title: Dominican Republic contracts first parametric insurance as part of its adaptive social protection system
content: The Dominican Republic has become the first country in Latin America and the Caribbean to integrate parametric insurance into its adaptive social protection system; providing rapid financial support to households when extreme rainfall or wind events occur

title: Europe’s summer weather risk outlook: What businesses need to prepare for now

title: Credit risk of floods in the UK is contained. For now.
content: Moody’s finds that increasing severity and frequency of hazards, and the sunsetting of the UK reinsurance plan Flood Re in 2039, could widen public sector exposure.
snippet: Moody’s finds that increasing severity and frequency of hazards, and the sunsetting of the UK reinsurance plan Flood Re in 2039, could widen public sector exposure.

title: Climate risks to Syria’s urban water and sanitation systems
content: This briefing paper presents key findings from a semi-quantitative climate risk assessment of Syria's urban drinking water and sanitation, intended to support the country's reconstruction efforts.
snippet: This briefing paper presents key findings from a semi-quantitative climate risk assessment of Syria's urban drinking water and sanitation, intended to support the country's reconstruction efforts.

title: How to manage crises differently in ASALs without talking about a nexus: what we can learn from the water sector

title: Addressing spiralling climate and environmental risks in Yemen's Taizz and Hadhramaut governates

title: Could this be Australia’s warmest winter ever?
content: Many parts of Australia have already broken early winter maximum and minimum temperature records.

title: Yale’s new extreme weather dashboard could help older adults understand climate health risks

title: Disasters destroy more than homes – they scatter communities too
content: Evidence from Uganda suggests that disasters can reduce income and life satisfaction for years, especially when households are displaced without their social networks.
snippet: Evidence from Uganda suggests that disasters can reduce income and life satisfaction for years, especially when households are displaced without their social networks.

title: A severe El Niño could threaten something essential to half of humanity – rice
content: Forecasters expect the El Niño now underway in the tropical Pacific to strengthen into a strong or very strong climate driver later this year.
snippet: Forecasters expect the El Niño now underway in the tropical Pacific to strengthen into a strong or very strong climate driver later this year.

title: Preventing and managing climate-related insecurity: lessons from the lake Chad basin regional strategy
content: The lives and livelihoods of local communities in the borderlands of the Lake Chad Basin are disrupted by both climate change and conflict , which are mutually reinforcing.

title: When metrics miss the point: why climate adaptation metrics must reflect plural worldviews

title: Zambia crisis and disaster risk financing diagnostic

title: Unbearable heat for hundreds of millions

title: ‘This was something new’: what people in Greenland told us about learning to live with wildfires

title: Risk analysis of infrastructure vulnerability and sustainable resource exploitation in the Arctic

title: A hotter, drier 2026 monsoon can compound disaster risks in the Hindu Kush Himalaya
content: <p>The HKH Monsoon Outlook 2026 forecasts that several countries in the Hindu Kush Himalaya (HKH) region, including Bhutan, India, Nepal, and Pakistan, are likely to witness drier-than-average monsoon conditions coupled with temperatures averaging 0.5&nbsp;°C to 2&nbsp;°C above the seasonal normal.&nbsp;</p><p>A below-normal monsoon with above-normal seasonal temperature may compound disaster vulnerabilities across the HKH region, warns scientists from the International Centre for Integrated Mountain Development (ICIMOD), a co-publisher of this year’s Outlook.&

title: Climate action as peace enabler: lessons from the Sahel on delivering peace and social dividends in fragile settings

title: Venezuela’s deadly earthquakes happened on a fault similar to the San Andreas, and the risks aren’t over yet – a geophysicist explains
content: University of Southern California geophysicist Sylvain Barbot explained what’s known about the earthquake pulses so far, what risks are still ahead and why Californians should pay attention.

title: Updated Return on Investment for preparedness to respond study
content: This report presents updated findings from WFP’s Return on Investment (ROI) for Preparedness study, demonstrating the value of pre-shock investments in strengthening emergency response.
